message.abstract:
<jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>BACKGROUND</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The risk factors for mortality of COVID-19 classified as critical type have not been well described.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>OBJECTIVES</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>This study aimed to described the clinical outcomes and further explored risk factors of in-hospital death for COVID-19 classified as critical type.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>METHODS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>This was a single-center retrospective cohort study. From February 5, 2020 to March 4, 2020, 98 consecutive patients classified as critical COVID-19 were included in Huo Shen Shan Hospital. The final date of follow-up was March 29, 2020. The primary outcome was all-cause mortality during hospitalization. Multivariable Cox regression model was used to explore the risk factors associated with in-hospital death.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>RESULTS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Of the 98 patients, 43 (43.9%) died in hospital, 37(37.8%) discharged, and 18(18.4%) remained in hospital. The mean age was 68.5 (63, 75) years, and 57 (58.2%) were female. The most common comorbidity was hypertension (68.4%), followed by diabetes (17.3%), angina pectoris (13.3%). Except the sex (Female: 68.8% vs 49.1%, P=0.039) and angina pectoris (20.9% vs 7.3%,
                    <jats:italic>P</jats:italic>
                    = 0.048), there was no difference in other demographics and comorbidities between non-survivor and survivor groups. The proportion of elevated alanine aminotransferase, creatinine, D-dimer and cardiac injury marker were 59.4%, 35.7%, 87.5% and 42.9%, respectively, and all showed the significant difference between two groups. The acute cardiac injury, acute kidney injury (AKI), and acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) were observed in 42.9%, 27.8% and 26.5% of the patients. Compared with survivor group, non-survivor group had more acute cardiac injury (79.1% vs 14.5%,
                    <jats:italic>P</jats:italic>
                    <0.0001), AKI (50.0% vs 10.9%,
                    <jats:italic>P</jats:italic>
                    <0.0001), and ARDS (37.2% vs 18.2%, P=0.034). Multivariable Cox regression showed increasing hazard ratio of in-hospital death associated with acute cardiac injury (HR, 6.57 [95% CI, 1.89-22.79]) and AKI (HR, 2.60 [95% CI, 1.15-5.86]).
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>CONCLUSIONS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>COVID-19 classified as critical type had a high prevalence of acute cardiac and kidney injury, which were associated with a higher risk of in-hospital mortality.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Clinical Course And Risk Factors For In-hospital Death In Critical COVID-19 In Wuhan, China
